reviews.review_msg: You don't need the textbook or to read the slides, just use the tutorial slides and the practice midterm given by the TA's to study. Both TAs are fantastic. His exams were so easy. Beware of high curve, almost everyone i know got curved down. Shafik as a prof is good but overhyped, his lectures are not that useful, tends to cover 4 slides a lect.

Shafik was kind and helpful. His exam was fair, he tested you on materials that were taught in lecture. His TA was also kind and helpful. I personally like this class because the course materials are fun. You don't only memorize but analyze too. Overall, I suggest you to take bus 393 with Shafik.
Shafik is a gem. Best professor I've ever had. Exams are a cakewalk. Don't really need the textbook. TAs provide plenty of review material so as long as you drill that there will be 0 issues.

Everyone I know has hyped this man up, but after taking a class with him, I was really disappointed. He likes to go on tangents during lectures and sometimes they're relevant, usually not. Slides and readings are disorganized, extremely unclear grading, oh and don't bother participating because he gives everyone a good participation grade anyway.

HE IS THE BEST PROF IN BEEDIE NGL
he will make everything easy, and he will give you many examples to make the concepts not that tough. 
Personally think he is the only prof worth me to pay such international tuition
Shafik is great! Pretty easy class, just study lecture/tutorial slides, do any practice exams provided and you'll do fine.

Brilliant! Brilliant! Brilliant! He is the only prof that I don't want him to end the lecture early!

The course has a tight curve so the final exam determines everything. Concepts are mostly discussed during class and he *will* test you on the final (e.g. Meiorin test, substratum doctrine). Know the presentation cases as well, they are testable.

Shafik's lectures are super interesting and he gives lots of good examples that stick with you for the exams (so you should attend lecture). The class is fairly easy with a light workload for a 300-level class but the tradeoff is high a average so you have to score high to get above the curve. Shafik was awesome and hope I can have him again.

Had him in BUS 303 B4, and took him for law too. He's a great prof, and I study for 3 hrs the morning of the exam and it's a breeze. Avgs are high tho, making it competitive to do well. Just get perfect on the tutorial, highest mark on the prez, and hopefully get above curve on the exam to secure the bag. Course is good w/ other tough quant classes

Taken 2 classes with him 393 & 303. Hands down, great prof. Super consistent, always prepared, & makes the material make sense. He doesn't just read off slides. Used tons of real-life examples. Also really funny, keeps the class from ever getting boring. But heads up — don't use your phone in his class.

Best course, best teacher at Beedie School. The class is One of the most exciting classes I have taken with this teacher. He makes the subject exciting and gets all of us involved in participating and critically analyzing cases and subjects. He does it in a way that is very inviting and fun. I love his classes. Class is always full.

He is a lawyer who teaches this course and so very well. I hate early morning classes but his I looked forward to. He make a subject that is so dry very “sexy” and exciting with real world examples from his experience. I love that he gives context and then hypotheticals that we can relate to. I am really thinking of going into law.

By far the best teacher I have come across. It is such a pleasure to be in his class. He makes the environment extremely fun to learn in, and he truly cares about seeing everyone succeed! I would 100% recommend taking his class.

Took BUS 303 with Shafik this semester. Grading criteria is unclear. He is arrogant and egotistical and does not hide it one bit. He always drifts off to random subjects, which is useless for students. So many examples taught in class were no use for studying. People recommend Shafik, but take it with anyone else, it's not a hard subject.

Excellent! Best in my three years at BEEDIE. Knows the material well. Using his book on the subject which has some great study materials. Love his Friday classes. He is very entertaining when teaching. Love his classes. He does not suffer lazy students and those using cellphones in his class.  Taking employment law with him  now

I have taken two courses with Shafik so far (BUS 233 and BUS393) both of which have been incredible. He is such an amazing prof with his humour, real life examples to connect to lectures, and stories from his career being a lawyer. I hope to take couple more courses with him for sure. There is a reason his classes fill up so fast and get full!
There are easier professors for this class.. for the sake of yourself, avoid him.

Shafik made lectures enjoyable. His exams are also very fair. The group project could use more structure/instructions. Will make plenty of jokes which I loved (not for the easily offended). Teaches using examples, which is how you will be evaluated. Will take more courses with him even though I don't need to.
Shafik is a great prof! He is a funny guy and teaches well. Exams are fair as well, just wish you're not on his bad books and don't get bullied every lecture.

There really isn't anything to study in this course. Getting an B+/A- is incredibly easy, but doing any better than that seems impossible because of the curve. He has a dry sense of humour, which is at times funny, and other times rude.
Engaging lectures and useful guidance from the prof. For study prep, ChatGPT and unstuckstudy ai were super helpful alongside the free course materials. Overall, a rewarding experience.

He is a good proffesor and there is absolutely no doubt about this. He knows his stuff very well, uses different examples from the class to explain the stuff. The average for the course is so high.  If I would get a chance to do this course again, I wont do with him at all so that I can get a better grade! If you want an A, dont take with him.

Loved the content of the class, and the mark I got was not bad. the issue here is the prof. extremely rude, ill mannered, and finds joy out of embarrassing his students. he should have some shame. dont ever book a phone call w him- he will use that time to degrade you

Super engaging and funny lectures. For the law assignment, he gives you a sample you can follow. For the ethics exam, he guides you on how to approach the questions that will be on the final. He also posts slides and lecture recordings. However, think before asking him for help. He doesn't like it if you email him late at night.

Good lectures and funny prof, but be warned; he is ideologically possessed, very old school, rude when you talk to him or approach for questions. Take James Pflanz instead, at least he doesn't yell at me over the phone when i request office hours.
